title: pilosella apatelia subsp. pratigena
intro: Pilosella apatelia subsp. pratigena, also known as a subspecies of the Plantain-leaved Hawk's-beard, is a flowering plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The medicinal properties of Pilosella apatelia subsp. pratigena include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as topical creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
